title: Public opinion dynamics regarding the Uber vs traditional taxi industry conflict in France during 2015

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Based on pre-protest baseline sentiment in IFOP archives
Estimated: Gradual shift following initial market entry awareness
Estimated: Impact of digital platform convenience narrative
Estimated: Continued growth in ride-sharing adoption

events.description: Massive taxi driver strikes across France blocking major airports and roads
French court rules against UberPop, fueling public debate on innovation vs regulation
Government announces new regulations for ride-sharing apps

agents.name: Uber France
agents.description: The ride-sharing platform advocating for market liberalization

agents.name: Taxi Unions (CGT/FO)
agents.description: Traditional taxi drivers' organizations protecting existing licenses

agents.name: French Government
agents.description: Regulators attempting to balance innovation and labor protection

agents.name: Urban Commuters
agents.description: Consumers favoring cheaper and more accessible transport

agents.name: Rural/Provincial Residents
agents.description: Less impacted by ride-sharing, generally indifferent or skeptical

agents.name: Tech Industry Lobbyists
agents.description: Advocates for digital economy and deregulation

notes: Pro-side (+1) is defined as support for Uber and ride-sharing services. Against-side (-1) is defined as support for traditional taxi industry protections. Data quality: IFOP survey provides the anchor for the final round; intermediate rounds are interpolated based on major news events and protest intensity.
